entry.title: Re: CPI connectivity with MS SQL Server on-prem using Cloud Connector
entry.content:
i am getting this error when i am trying to send this body in content modifier: 
use <database_name>;

error details: org.springframework.jdbc.CannotGetJdbcConnectionException: Failed to obtain JDBC Connection; nested exception is com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The connection string contains a badly formed name or value., cause: com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The connection string contains a badly formed name or value.

entry.title: SQL Database Connection
entry.content:
Good day,

I am using the SAC free trial and want to connect to an SQL database to extract data and create stories. However, I am encountering the following error while establishing the connection.

Error: A location with an attached SAP Analytics Cloud agent must be configured to create a SQL Database connection. Please contact your administrator or go to the Data Source Configuration tab in System Administration to ensure a location is configured correctly

Also, is it possible to get admin access in the free trial?

Best Regards,

Keshav M

entry.title: Re: ITSM error in SOLMAN_SETUP T-Code
entry.content: Hi Sriram, Thank you for your response. I have reviewed the note you suggested, and I can confirm that there is no issue with the SID. However, there appears to be a mismatch in the LMDB technical details—specifically with the technical instance name, hostname, and ports. We recently migrated the Solution Manager system, but the LMDB still reflects the old system details. Could you please advise on how to update or correct this information?

entry.title: Message No. 00058
entry.content:
Hi,

Entry APOH   does not exist in T001K (check entry)

Message No. 00058

I have created material type APOH . But when I am goint to check via mm01 that timei am facing the issue .

Please tell me , how Can I solve this error in SAP MM

entry.title: Publishing to a Queue or Topic in BTP Event Mesh using Postman
entry.content:
Dear Professional.

Currently, I am creating and setting up BTP's Event Mesh Instance (only messagingrest method is activated) and testing in Cockpit, all (Publish - Queue/Topic, Cosume) are processed normally, but an error occurs when requesting Publish (Queue and Topic) in Postman.

When testing Publish (Queue) in Postman, a 403 Error occurs. I don't know what the problem is.

The URI called is as follows.
https://<http rest address>/messagingrest/v1/queues/sap%2Fdemo%2Fai/messages

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Vendor invoice
entry.content:
Hello @Kavita91 open your GL in FS00 T-code with company code & go to tab "create/bank/interest" check if indicator "Post automatically only" ticked if yes remove it, if this is not needed.

Ideally for inventory GL (OBYC) or GL's setup in VKOA this indicator should be ticked.

Regards,

Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Sync SF - Microsoft Entra - Multiple E...
entry.content:
Hi Sascha,

have a look into the SF Attribute List, e.g. jobTitle. Attached you'll find an example to make adecission between primary and secondary assignment.

Default, this fetch the first in List, which is not always the primary Assignement.

$.employmentNav.results[0].jobInfoNav.results[0].jobTitle

Change it to fetch the primary Assignment

$.employmentNav.results[?(@.userNav.isPrimaryAssignment == true)].jobInfoNav.results[0].jobTitle

If a user changed from contingent worker into permanent, you can use following attribute and use it in an expression.

$.employmentNav.results[?(@.userNav.isPrimaryAssignment == true)].isContingentWorker

 

Regards

Christian

entry.title: Re: how to avoid COMMIT WORK in sending mail using FM
entry.content:
Hi,

how did you solve this problem please

entry.title: Re: Cancel Mat Doc Error - Deficit of MCHB-CLABS 24,956 L...
entry.content: The error message FI149 is from FI area - you should adjust the question tags or open a new question. Check the following notes:

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Table Reading in SAP ABAP & Pricing Integration
entry.content: Hi Daniel, First of all, thank you for your detailed and constructive feedback — it’s genuinely appreciated. Let me address your points one by one: the use of prefixes like lt_ and lr_ in ABAP is more about convention. Typically, lt_ is used for internal tables, whereas lr_ is reserved for reference-type variables. Since I’m working with internal tables in this context (e.g., field catalog), using lt_ is appropriate. Regarding the multiple calls to DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET, in this particular case, different table names are passed dynamically, which makes it necessary to call the function for each one. Of course, if the table names were static, caching the field info would definitely be more efficient — and you’re absolutely right in pointing that out. On the topic of DFIES vs. DD03P, the confusion might stem from mixing runtime and dictionary structures. DFIES is better suited for runtime scenarios, while DD03P is more appropriate when working directly with the data dictionary. Lastly, your point about using DELETE inside a LOOP is very valid. Modifying the table while iterating over it can lead to index shifting issues. A safer approach is to collect the records to be deleted in a separate table and then delete them outside the loop. Overall, your questions are very relevant and thoughtful — and there’s absolutely no need to apologize. It's clear you're eager to learn, and I truly respect that. Thanks again for your input, and best of luck in your development journey!

entry.title: Re: Calculating cost using formula in work center.
entry.content:
Hello everybody ,

I am facing the same issue in PP, i assigned a 1$/H to an activity and 10$/H for another, when assigning respectively a 1H & 8H workin schedule the system calculates 0,75 $ and 60$ (its deducting 1/4 of the value).

Any ideas ?

entry.title: Implementing Event-Driven Architecture with Canonical Data Model in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Event-Driven Architecture (EDA):
Event-Driven Architecture is a design pattern in which system components communicate by producing and consuming events. An event represents a change of state (e.g., a new sales order, product update). In EDA, producers send events without knowing who consumes them, enabling loose coupling between systems.

Key benefits:

  • Decoupling

  • Real-time data flow

  • Scalability and flexibility

    Canonical Data Model (CDM):
    A Canonical Data Model is a standardized format for data exchange between systems. Instead of each system mapping data differently, a single unified model is used across all integrations.

    The Integration Flow implementation looks like this:



    Step 1: Prepare Canonical Data

  • Prepare your canonical XML/JSON payload. Here’s a sample XML code:

<Root>
	<Products>
		<Product>
			<Price>2.5</Price>
			<Rating>4</Rating>
			<DiscontinuedDate/>
			<ID>0</ID>
			<ReleaseDate>1992-01-01T00:00:00.000</ReleaseDate>
		</Product>
	</Products>
	<Categories>
		<Category>
			<ID>0</ID>
			<Name>Food</Name>
		</Category>
	</Categories>
	<Suppliers>
		<Supplier>
			<Address>
				<State>WA</State>
				<ZipCode>98074</ZipCode>
				<Street>NE 228th</Street>
				<Country>USA</Country>
				<City>Sammamish</City>
			</Address>
			<Concurrency>0</Concurrency>
			<ID>0</ID>
			<Name>Exotic Liquids</Name>
		</Supplier>
	</Suppliers>
	<Employee>
		<Name>Hirva</Name>
		<ID>4</ID>
	</Employee>
	<Book>
		<Book_ID>101</Book_ID>
		<Book_Title>Effective Java</Book_Title>
		<Book_Author>Joshua Bloch</Book_Author>
		<Book_Description>A comprehensive guide to best practices in Java programming.</Book_Description>
	</Book>
	<Customers>
		<Customer>
			<Number>1</Number>
			<FirstName>Fred</FirstName>
			<LastName>Landis</LastName>
			<Address>
				<Street>Oakstreet</Street>
				<City>Boston</City>
				<ZIP>23320</ZIP>
				<State>MA</State>
			</Address>
		</Customer>
	</Customers>
	<breakfast_menu>
		<food>
			<name>Belgian Waffles</name>
			<price>$5.95</price>
			<description>Two of our famous Belgian Waffles with plenty of real maple syrup</description>
			<calories>650</calories>
		</food>
	</breakfast_menu>
</Root>

Step 2: Send Data from Postman

  • Method: POST

  • URL: Your SAP CPI iFlow endpoint (e.g., https://<tenant>.iflmap.sap.hana.ondemand.com/http/canonicalEvent)

  • Header: Content-Type: application/xml

    Body: Paste the sample canonical message.

    Step 3: Build SAP CPI iFlow

    Component Purpose

    HTTPS Sender AdapterReceives HTTP POST from Postman
    Content Modifier (optional)Logs or adds initial data
    Router (Content-Based)Routes based on Type (SalesOrder, Product)
    JMS Receiver AdapterSends message to the target JMS queue (SalesOrderQueue, ProductQueue)
    parallel multicastAllows you to send the same message to multiple routes for parallel processing

    Router Conditions:

    If using XML XPath:

    • Condition 1 (Sales Order):
      //CanonicalMessage/Type = 'SalesOrder'

    • Condition 2 (Product):
      //CanonicalMessage/Type = 'Product'

    Step 4: Configure JMS Queues

    1.In SAP CPI Cockpit:

    • Go to JMS Resources

    • Create Queues:

      • SalesOrderQueue

      • ProductQueue

    2.In CPI iFlow:

    • Add JMS Receiver Adapter

    • Point each route to its respective queue.

    Step 5: Deploy & Test

    1.Deploy the iFlow.
    2.Post Data using Postman.
    3.Check Monitoring:

    • In SAP CPI Message Monitor, confirm successful routing.

    • In JMS Queue Monitor, confirm that the message lands in the correct queue.

    Error Handling: Sending Email on Exception


    Steps to Implement Email Notification in Error Handling:

    1. Add Exception Subprocess:
    In your main iFlow:

    • Drag and drop an Exception Subprocess (gray dotted box around your main flow).

    • This subprocess will automatically catch any unhandled exceptions in the main flow

    2.Add Error Handling Components:

    Component Purpose

    Start Event (Escalation)Starts the exception subprocess
    Content ModifierPrepares email content (subject, body, etc.)
    Mail Receiver AdapterSends the actual email


    3.Configure the Content Modifier (Prepare Email):

    Exchange Property:

    • EmailSubject: Error in CPI iFlow: Canonical Event Router

    • EmailBody: {As like you want}

      4. Configure Mail Receiver Adapter:

    • Protocol: Mail

    • SMTP Server: Your mail server (e.g., smtp.office365.com)

    • Authentication: Yes (configure in Security Materials)

    • To: e.g., support@yourcompany.com

    • Subject: ${property.EmailSubject}

    • Body: ${property.EmailBody}

    Make sure your CPI tenant has Mail (SMTP) connectivity set up.

    5. Deploy & Test:

    • Force an error: You can test by deliberately sending invalid data from Postman.

    • Check:

      • CPI Monitoring (Exception Subprocess should trigger)

      • Email inbox (verify error notification received)

entry.title: Re: how to make alv editable for any two fields& add save...
entry.content:
For own buttons have a look at sample program SALV_DEMO_TABLE_FUNCTIONS.

Perform an internet search to find editable SALV.

entry.title: ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI - DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR
entry.content:
I am trying to create info records with conditions using the ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI function module. When I execute the function module manually via SE37, it works perfectly. However, when I run it through my report with the exact same data, I encounter an update error when BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT is called.

Upon debugging, I found that the error occurs because the function module RV_KONDITION_SICHERN_V13A  is being called twice within an update task. The second call attempts to insert the same key combination into table A017 that was already inserted during the first run. Since this key combination already exists, it causes a dump.

It's important to note that I only have one condition in my info record.

Further investigation in the debugger revealed two spots where RV_KONDITION_SICHERN_V13A is called:

  1. Inside ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI, the first call happens in the ME_POST_INFORECORD  subroutine (PERFORM BUCHEN), which triggers RV_CONDITION_SAVE.

     

  2. After ME_POST_INFORECORD, the method post_conditions()  is called, which also triggers RV_CONDITION_SAVE in the update task.

When I execute the function module in SE37, the first call does not trigger the condition save, and 

cl_mmpur_bapi_po=>check_repid_loaded( 'ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI==FT' )

 returns abap_true because ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI==FT exists in the call stack. As a result, the function proceeds without entering RV_CONDITION_SAVE, where  RV_KONDITION_SICHERN_V13A is called.

Call stack from se37:

 

Call stack from se38 (my custom report):

 

Any ideas on how to resolve it?

entry.title: SAP Datasphere graphical view deployment issue
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have a graphical view in SAP Datasphere, which works fine. I have changed it many times and everything was fine. Suddenly I've got the error message: "Model could not be saved. Unexpected token 'c', "csn.query.column" is not valid JSON" or "Model could not be deployed. {"message":"Unexpected token 'c', \"csn.query.column\" is not valid JSON"}" depends on what I want to do save or deploy.

I can't save or deploy the view. I can't restore old version because of the same error (even if it's really old version). I've checked all predcessor views - everything is fine. When copy SQL code from this view to new SQL-view and try to deploy - everything is fine. If I do Save as new view and deploy it - it also works fine.

It's also affected successor views - they have the same errors. The last thing I did before the issue was changing size of measure with type decimal. I don't understand is it one time bug, so I just need to create a new view and replace all occurences of old view with this one, or do I have some issue which can break my model in future again.

entry.title: PO Creation and Approval timing is wrongly showing in the approval workflow
entry.content: In reference to the attached image of the PO approval process, we observed that after Mr. Raja Gunasekaran’s approval, the workflow proceeded to Mr. Pasupathy for further approval. However, there appears to be an unexpected intervention by Mr. Sumanth, as the system shows he created a task in between these steps.Upon checking with Mr. Sumanth, he has confirmed that he did not make any changes to the PO. Additionally, both Mr. Raja’s approval and the task creation attributed to Mr. Sumanth are timestamped at 10:11, indicating they occurred simultaneously.

entry.title: PO Creation and Approval timing is wrongly showing in the approval workflow
entry.content: In reference to the attached image of the PO approval process, we observed that after Mr. Raja Gunasekaran’s approval, the workflow proceeded to Mr. Pasupathy for further approval. However, there appears to be an unexpected intervention by Mr. Sumanth, as the system shows he created a task in between these steps.Upon checking with Mr. Sumanth, he has confirmed that he did not make any changes to the PO. Additionally, both Mr. Raja’s approval and the task creation attributed to Mr. Sumanth are timestamped at 10:11, indicating they occurred simultaneously.

entry.title: AnalyticalTable: Multiple Requests on Scroll and Data Not Bound When Count Is Missing in the result
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I'm working with sap.ui.table.AnalyticalTable in an SAPUI5 application and currently facing two issues related to scrolling behavior and data binding.

Issue 1: Multiple Backend Requests on Scroll

When a user scrolls to the bottom of the AnalyticalTable, multiple backend requests are triggered to fetch the next set of records. Ideally, I expect only one request per scroll interaction to load the next chunk of data.

  • I have set the threshold property on the table.

  • The table is bound to an OData v2 service.

  • Grouping is enabled on one of the columns.

Questions:

  • Why are multiple requests being triggered on scroll?

  • Is there a way to restrict this behavior so that only one request is made per scroll?

Issue 2: Data Not Bound When __count Is Missing

Due to performance considerations, I tried omitting the __count value from the backend response. While the data is successfully returned from the backend, it does not get bound to the AnalyticalTable.

Here is a sample of the current response returned from the Node.js service:

return {
d: {
results: data || [],
__count: 10000
}
};

If I remove the __count from the response, the table fails to display the data even though the records are present in the payload.

Question:

  • Is __count mandatory for AnalyticalTable to bind and display data correctly?

  • Are there any recommended workarounds for scenarios where the count is intentionally omitted and scroll should work ?

Any insights or guidance on how to resolve these issues would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI - DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR
entry.content:
I am trying to create info records with conditions using the ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI function module. When I execute the function module manually via SE37, it works perfectly. However, when I run it through my report with the exact same data, I encounter an update error when BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT is called.

Upon debugging, I found that the error occurs because the function module RV_KONDITION_SICHERN_V13A  is being called twice within an update task. The second call attempts to insert the same key combination into table A017 that was already inserted during the first run. Since this key combination already exists, it causes a dump.

It's important to note that I only have one condition in my info record.

Further investigation in the debugger revealed two spots where RV_KONDITION_SICHERN_V13A is called:

  1. Inside ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI, the first call happens in the ME_POST_INFORECORD  subroutine (PERFORM BUCHEN), which triggers RV_CONDITION_SAVE.

  2. After ME_POST_INFORECORD, the method post_conditions()  is called, which also triggers RV_CONDITION_SAVE in the update task.

The key difference is when I execute the function module in SE37, the first call skipped and does not trigger the condition save, and 

cl_mmpur_bapi_po=>check_repid_loaded( 'ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI==FT' )

 returns abap_true because ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI==FT exists in the call stack. As a result, the function proceeds without entering RV_CONDITION_SAVE, where  RV_KONDITION_SICHERN_V13A is called.

Call stack from se37:

Call stack from se38 (my custom report):

 Any ideas on how to resolve it?

entry.title: Re: Mandatory Data Types
entry.content:
Hello A.,

You can safely proceed with other imports in correct order of Employee Central User creation without performing Compensation Information data import.
2080146 - What is the Correct Import Order When Creating Users? - Employee Central

  •  Perform these imports in the order mentioned below.
    1. Basic Import
    2. Biographical Information Import
    3. Employment Details Import
    4. Job Information Import
    5. Personal Information Import

Compensation information import is at the last 6th step of this order, hence you may skip it.

However, it is mandatory to keep Compensation Information and all other HRIS element enabled in your data model even if you are not using it.
2586311 - Manage Business Configuration: Which hris-elements must be enabled and configured for Employee Central
Note: Any element that is enabled must have at least one field enabled in that element. If you do not, App error's will occur in the hire wizards

 

Regards,

Razia

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance in the Balkans: Albania, Croatia, Romania, Serbia & Greece
entry.content:
As e-invoicing mandates continue to spread across Europe, the Balkan countries are increasingly aligning with EU directives by introducing comprehensive digital fiscal frameworks. To support customers operating in this region, CRIMSONSAP has developed a specialized SAP Document and Reporting Compliance partner solution for the Balkans, compatible with ECC, S/4HANA On-Premise, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud (both private and public editions).

This blog provides an overview of the legal mandates in the Balkan countries and highlights how the SAP Document Reporting Compliance for the region enables compliance through an integrated, scalable architecture.

Legal Mandates – Digitalisation in the Balkans

The Balkans have taken varied approaches to digital tax compliance, with several countries already enforcing e-invoicing requirements and others preparing to go live in the coming years. Below is a summary of the current status:

🇦🇱Albania

  • B2G: Mandatory since Jan 1, 2021

  • B2B: Mandatory since Jul 1, 2021

  • B2C: Mandatory from Jan 1, 2025

  • eWaybill: Implemented via RO e-Transport from Jul 1, 2022

Albania’s fiscalization system follows a phased model and adopts EN 16931 as the default technical standard.

Both eInvoicing & eWaybill are legally required with QR Codes and Legal Numbering.

🇧🇬Bulgaria

Regulatory Change Manager : Bulgaria 

Currently Bulgaria is moving ahead with mandatory SAFT and has announced the intention to rollout an electronic invoicing system for all by 2028.

  • eTransport: Introduced in Oct 2023, with extended scope from Jan 2024 for certain goods movements

🇧🇦Bosnia and Herzegovina

  • Draft legislation was released in Nov 2024, with public consultation held in early 2025. The official rollout timeline is pending parliamentary approval.

  • The expected solution is a clearance model with QR Code.
  • Though optional out network already operates in Bosnia
  • We also provide support for eVAT

🇭🇷Croatia

Regulatory Change Manager : Croatia 

  • B2G: Mandatory since Jul 1, 2019

  • B2B: Mandatory from Jan 1, 2026

  • B2C: Mandatory from Jan 1, 2026

🇲🇪Montenegro

  • Montenegro has licensed the same solution as Serbia

  • Projected to implement mandatory B2G and B2B e-invoicing during 2025–2026

🇷🇴Romania

Regulatory Change Manager : Romania 

  • B2G: Mandatory since Jul 1, 2022

  • B2B and B2C: Fully mandatory from Jan 1, 2025

  • eTransport: Operational since Jul 1, 2022

🇷🇸Serbia

Regulatory Change Manager : Serbia 

  • B2G: Mandatory since May 1, 2022

  • G2B: Mandatory since Jul 1, 2022

  • B2B: Mandatory since Jan 1, 2023

  • eTransport: Mandatory from Jan 1, 2026 with additional obligations from Oct 1, 2027

Our solution is compliant for both AR & AP plus eTransport.

🇸🇮Slovenia

Regulatory Change Manager : Slovenia 

  • B2G: Mandatory since Jan 2015

  • B2B: Postponed to Jan 2027 based on a new draft law

  • B2C: No current mandate

CrimsonSAP Balkans Network is hosted and already live in Slovenia and can enable you to be prepared early.

🇸🇰Slovakia

Regulatory Change Manager : Slovakia 

  • B2G: Mandatory since Apr 1, 2023

  • B2B/B2C: Mandatory from Jan 1, 2027

  • Cross-border B2B: Mandatory from Jul 1, 2030 under the EU ViDA initiative

🇬🇷Greece

  • B2G: Mandatory from Jan 1, 2025

  • B2B: Authorized to be mandatory from Jul 1, 2025

  • B2C: Currently voluntary

Are you ready for additional changes coming to Greece?

If you still use MyDATA Solution to send Tax Documents and Non Customer Invoices this must also be sent via a certified Provider as your B2B invoices must.

Greece is also introducing eDelivery Notes via MyDATA

If you wish to discuss Greece drop us a message as our solution is already partnered with a Greek Provider and integrated to Document Reporting Compliance via our Balkans Network.

SAP DRC Balkans Extension – Scalable Architecture

To support these country-specific mandates, CRIMSONSAP offers a regional solution powered by SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition. The architecture features:

  • End-to-End Integration with Tax Authorities

  • Unified UBL 2.3 Format supporting multiple document types:

    • eInvoices (FI, SD, FICA, RE-FX)

    • Correction & Self-billing Invoices

    • eWaybills & eDelivery Note

  • Outbound & Inbound Processing

  • Support for PEPPOL and API Connectivity

  • DRC-based Storage of Response Files and Approval Data

This extension runs on the SAP Business Technology Platform and provides seamless connectivity via a certified cloud connector, reducing the need for individual country customizations.

Key Capabilities

  • Electronic invoice generation in XML (SD/FI)

  • Manual or automated invoice submission

  • Integration with external systems (e.g., VIM)

  • Inbound invoice receipt and processing

  • Conversion of XML to human-readable formats (PDF, HTML)

  • Synchronous feedback from tax authorities

Implementation Benefits

Our solution uses a single proprietary UBL format that means subsequent countries require only extension of the UBL to each country and extension if company codes differ slightly.

  • Fast Time to Value – Go live in 4–5 weeks

  • Single Strategic Integration – One point of connection across Balkan nations

  • Compliance Assurance – Country-specific XML validated, approved & stored in Document Reporting Compliance with synchronous responses between your SAP & Tax Authority's

  • Cost Efficiency – Avoid costly technical upgrades per country

  • Future Proofing – Scalable framework ready for upcoming mandates

Conclusion

The Balkan region is rapidly evolving toward full-scale digital tax compliance. CRIMSONSAP’s SAP Document Reporting Compliance partner solution for the Balkans offers a future-ready, scalable, and compliant solution that supports businesses in navigating this complex landscape with confidence.

Our network also integrates to PEPPOL, Tungsten and other large Networks and can manage your electronic ordering replacing traditional EDI connections.

For more details or to request a demo, visit www.crimsonsap.com

entry.title: Re: What is the best way to have an external API make a U...
entry.content:
Hmm, never heard about a SAP remote desktop connection?! (I only know Microsoft's...)

But the Python RFC component supports the same connections parameters as the C RFC SDK, which for WebSocket-RFC are the hostname & port of the target endpoint, and optionally parameters for an HTTP Proxy, if the connection needs to be tunneled through a proxy. So if this RDC components acts like a proxy, you might try setting the RDC host & port in the proxy settings?!

entry.title: How to check if customer invoice is paid in ByD via OData
entry.content:
Dear SAP community,

I have a problem finding the correct Business Object name and field while configuring an OData service in SAP ByDesign.

I’m trying to check whether the client’s invoice is open or cleared. I found this article, but it didn’t help me much:https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-check-if-customer-invoice-is-paid-in-byd-via-webservice/qaq-p/12506714

I’m not sure which Business Object (BO) it should be. Has anyone else encountered this issue before?

To make things clearer, I’m including some screenshots below showing the exact location where the status is displayed.

 

In the OData Configuration Tool, I tried to select the BO name CustomerInvoice, but it doesn’t seem to be the correct one.

Can someone please assist me with this?

Best wishes!

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire 2025

Dear Valued Customers,

As we gear up for Sapphire 2025, the anticipation is palpable within the SAP Community. This year promises to be a transformative experience with a mix of onsite and virtual events tailored to cater to our diverse global community. Mark your calendars and prepare to dive into a world of groundbreaking innovations, visionary discussions, and networking opportunities.

Join our annual flagship event to discover how SAP helps businesses to navigate uncertainty with confidence and achieve sustainable success.

  • Hear from industry leaders using SAP to tackle today’s biggest challenges

  • Get a first look at the latest updates to SAP Business Suite and Joule Agents

  • Dive into real product demos, advanced analytics, and intelligent apps

Plus, you'll have the opportunity to attend exclusive keynotes and preconference seminars and engage in celebration night experiences.

Event Details:

Sapphire Orlando: May 19-21, 2025
Orlando will be hosting the flagship event, bringing together industry leaders, SAP executives, and thousands of attendees from around the world. Set against the lively backdrop of Orlando, this event will offer an immersive in-person experience with hands-on workshops, keynotes from thought leaders, and opportunities to explore SAP’s latest technological advancements. The conference takes place at the Orange County Convention Center (OCCC), North and South Concourse, in Orlando.

Sapphire Madrid: May 26-28, 2025
Following the Orlando event, Sapphire will journey to Madrid, captivating Europe with its unparalleled influence and insights. Madrid’s vibrant culture and business dynamism set the perfect stage for SAP’s innovative showcase. Expect engaging sessions focusing on digital transformation, sustainability, and business agility, tailored specifically for the European market. The conference takes place at the IFEMA Madrid, Av. del Partenón, 5, Barajas, 28042 Madrid, Spain (Puerta Norte, North Entrance)

Sapphire Virtual: May 20-21, 2025
For those unable to attend in person, the Sapphire Virtual event offers a comprehensive digital experience, bridging the gap for global participants. This event will ensure access to live-streamed sessions, virtual networking lounges, and exclusive interviews with industry pioneers - all from the comfort of your own home or office.

Why Attend?

Sapphire 2025 offers a unique platform to gain firsthand knowledge about SAP's roadmap and future strategies. It’s an unparalleled opportunity to network with peers, learn from experts, and explore solutions that can drive your business forward. Whether you're in the early stages of digital transformation or seeking advanced solutions to optimize processes, Sapphire 2025 is the place to be.

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to be at the forefront of business innovation. Whether you join us in Orlando, Madrid, or virtually, Sapphire 2025 promises to be an event to remember. Stay tuned for more updates on the SAP Community page.

Take the opportunity and register here for the Sapphire 2025!

Any Questions?

For general questions, email us at: SAPSapphireOrlando@sap.com

For registration or payment questions, email us at: support@sapevents.com

For housing questions, email us at: sapsapphire@conferencedirect.com

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: Re: Adding additional row in adobe form in SAP
entry.content: I Find the answer, that is Floating Field.

entry.title: Re: Foreign currency USD purchased at a certain rate at t...
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, you can post using the fixed rate by entering foreign and local currency amounts manually in the bank statement processing app or embedding the rate in your electronic bank statement file. The system will compute the exchange rate from these inputs.

Recommended Approach
To ensure the fixed rate is used during bank statement posting:

Use External Bank Statement with Fixed Exchange Rate
If you're using the "Manage Incoming/Outgoing Bank Statements" app or automatic bank statement processing (MT940, CAMT.053 format), the exchange rate must be embedded in the bank statement file (in fields such as Exchange Rate in CAMT.053).

SAP will use the exchange rate from the uploaded bank statement, overriding the standard rate from OB08.

Manual Entry (Postprocess Bank Statement Items)
Use the “Postprocess Bank Statement Items” app (F1866A) if manual intervention is needed. In this app:

Select the line item for USD purchase.

In the processing screen, manually enter both the foreign amount (USD) and local amount (EUR)
→ SAP automatically calculates and stores the correct exchange rate based on the ratio of these two values.

Note: Standard bank posting apps do not have a direct “exchange rate” input field, but the system derives the exchange rate from the foreign and local currency amounts you provide.

Ensure that both USD and EUR accounts are maintained correctly in your bank master data.

If exchange differences arise due to mismatch with OB08 rates, the system posts automatically to exchange gain/loss accounts (configured in OB09 / SSCUI 100297).

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/dc6a0c8a5b934aedaac8e2de3691626a.html

Let me know if you need more support

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Foreign currency USD purchased at a certain rate at t...
entry.content: Hello Chuma, Thank you very much for the quick reply! i will check it out thank you very much best regards N.S.

entry.title: Re: Hide standard Create /Delete button on loading RAP ap...
entry.content:
Hello ,

By default you don't want these Buttons ?

If Yes, then just removing them from the Behavior Definition(BDEF) on top of Projection View should work, but if you want based on some runtime Context then "features : instance" addition should help here.

Check the above points with respect to Behavior Definition.

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: ABAP EXPORT CHART AS IMAGE FROM EXCEL
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I need to export a chart as an image from an Excel file generated by ABAP.

I'm currently generating the Excel file with the chart using the abap2xlsx library. Everything works fine — the chart is created correctly in the `.xlsx` file.

Now, I want to automatically export this chart as an image (e.g., PNG or JPG).

I tried injecting a VBA macro manually into the file and it works perfectly when I open the Excel file and run the macro myself.

However, what I really want is to do this directly from ABAP:
- Either by embedding and executing a macro programmatically,
- Or using OLE2 automation to open the file and run the macro.

I’m looking for a clean way to automate this entire process from ABAP, without manual steps.

Has anyone done this before? Is there any reliable way to embed and execute VBA macros in Excel files generated by abap2xlsx?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Error 'cannot cancel document with a down payment tha...
entry.content:
Hi,

It is not possible because when the AP credit memo is added, the entry is reversed and the AP invoice is closed. Therefore, the AP invoice needs to be re-entered.

entry.title: Do we have a standard CDS view to create an external API to read bank statement of camt Format?
entry.content:
Dear Expert's,

Can you please suggest is there any standard CDS view to create an external API to read bank statement of CAMT Format?

For we need to opt for Mapping Procedure.

 

Thanks & Regards

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
@vikas_saparam1 

  • As the documentation says, it is only relevant for production client
  • If the test client is set to "Changes allowed", yes. If the test client is set to "No changes allowed" you need to remove the transport object related checks from the RAP behavior, see blog text
  • For ABAP cloud the combination "Manual" and "adaptableInProduction" as true is not allowed.
    The transport setting is as far as I know only relevant for the maintenance transaction / app. Therefore you should be able to record a configuration entry on a customizing request with transaction SE09 even if the transport setting is set to "no transport"

entry.title: Re: How do you get the infotype numbers to show in the ad...
entry.content: If you are able to change the infoset for the query, then those are the field group's text you need to modify.

entry.title: Re: Can the Inventory valuation for sales order stock be...
entry.content:
Hello @Grace_Li ,

Thank you, too, for your feedback.

You can utilise the Manage Your Solution app to access Self-Service Configuration UIs (SSCUI).

Steps:

  • Open the Manage Your Solution app.
  • Navigate to Configure Your Solution.
  • Search for and select the SSCUI related to Account Assignment Groups for Materials.
  • Here, you can view and assign predefined account assignment groups to materials.

Let me know, if you need further support

With regards

Chuma

One of the things that was most difficult was the difference between technical and functional. I don't know if you understand what I mean... The beauty of programming is how things can be done in many different ways.

entry.title: Re: "SAPSetFilterComponent shows #N/A on most sheets in AO workbook"
entry.content: #N/A is the result of an Excel formula in the cell. If you see the formula, what is it? Can you evaluate where the error is? (Use Excel menu Formulas > Evaluate Formula for instance)

Regards

Frans

entry.title: Time to migrate your Belize theme on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment
entry.content:
In this blog post, I will guide you through the process of migrating your custom theme based on Belize to a theme based on Quartz or Horizon.

Background

The Belize theme has been deprecated since SAPUI5 version 1.120. As of SAPUI5 version 1.135, the Belize theme has reached its end-of-life and is no longer supported for SAPUI5 applications. For more details, refer to the documentation on Supported Combinations of Themes and Libraries and the announcements Removal of Belize Theme of SAP Fiori and Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal.

Why Is It Important to migrate themes based on Belize?

With the removal of the Belize theme in SAPUI5, custom themes based on Belize will no longer be applied to SAPUI5 applications. Instead, the applications will default to the Horizon theme, which may not align with your desired branding.

Prerequisites

  • You have a subaccount on the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment and have created a custom theme based on Belize.
  • You are using the custom theme for applications running on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment, or you have created a communication scenario for the UI theme designer in S/4HANA Cloud (communication scenario SAP_COM_0623).

Step 1: Migrate the Theme

  1. Open the UI theme designer in the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment.
  2. Select the theme that is based on Belize.
  3. Click the "Migrate" button to migrate your theme. For detailed instructions, refer to Migrating Themes.

Step 2: Test and Adjust the Migrated Theme

  1. Edit the migrated theme in the UI theme designer. For detailed instructions, refer to Editing Themes.
  2. Test the theme using the available preview pages.
  3. Adjust any parameters as needed to achieve the same look and feel as before.
  4. If you have implemented custom CSS, verify that it still works as expected.
    Note: Theming capabilities have been enhanced for the Horizon theme, offering more options for customization. Consider replacing custom CSS with theming parameters to ensure your theme is more robust and compatible with future UI technology upgrades

Step 3: Publish Your Theme

  1. Rename the old theme based on Belize. For detailed instructions, refer to Renaming Themes.
  2. Rename the migrated theme to use the same theme ID as before. This ensures that no changes are required for users to continue using the theme.
  3. Publish your theme to make it available for use. For detailed instructions, refer to Publishing Themes.

Step 4: Test Your Applications

  1. Test your applications thoroughly with the migrated theme to ensure everything works as expected.

Step 5: Delete the Belize-Based Theme

  1. Once you have confirmed that the migrated theme works correctly, you can safely delete the old theme based on Belize. For detailed instructions, refer to Deleting Themes.

Recap

By following these steps, you can ensure a smooth transition from the deprecated Belize theme to a modern theme based on Quartz or Horizon, while maintaining a consistent user experience across your applications.

For additional information, refer to the linked guides. If you need further assistance, feel free to reach out via component CA-UI2-THD.

entry.title: Re: ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI - DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR
entry.content: Your screenshots are tiny, impossible to read the texts inside. Programs named XXXXXXXXX==FT are those generated to test the function module XXXXXXXXX in SE37.

entry.title: Transitioning from SAP BW to SAP Datasphere: Key Challenges You Should Know
entry.content:
As SAP continues its push toward cloud-based analytics with SAP Datasphere, many organizations are considering or actively moving away from SAP BW (Business Warehouse)—a platform they've trusted for years. While the promise of modern, cloud-native architecture and tighter integration with data lakes and external sources is compelling, the transition from a mature, stable system like BW to Datasphere isn't always smooth. This blog aims to caution users about the critical differences, limitations, and challenges you might face during or after this shift and going forward. If you're considering or planning a move, these insights can help you make informed decisions and avoid disruptions.

  1. Feature Parity is Still Evolving

SAP BW is a mature, Well-tested solution with rich features such as complex transformations, process chains, BEx queries. Many of these capabilities are either missing, limited, or work differently in SAP Datasphere.

  • No full replacement for BEx Queries – Datasphere's modeling  focused more on consumption by SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • No direct support for ABAP-based logic – Complex custom logic using ABAP routines has no native counterpart in Datasphere.
  • Limited process orchestration: Unlike BW’s process chains, Datasphere lacks a mature orchestration framework. The current "Data Integration Monitoring" interface is unintuitive and insufficient. Administrator may face extreme difficulties in tracking data packets transferred from source to target, identifying the next scheduled process, or monitoring delta loads effectively.  The job start and end date and time is confusing in the load process . 
  • Additionally, Replication Flows used for change data capture (e.g., from S/4HANA) often run continuously for 24 hours in a batch, placing significant and sustained load on the source system—slowing it down. This is a recurring issue in real-world implementations.
  •  
  • 2) Performance Can Be Unpredictable

In BW, performance tuning has been refined over decades. Datasphere, being a cloud solution, introduces new variables like virtual data access, latency, and network bottlenecks.

Remote table access (e.g., to S/4HANA or SQL Server ) can be slower, even for relatively smaller  datasets or views.

Limited visibility into backend processes makes troubleshooting harder than in BW

3) Security and Authorizations Are Different

SAP Datasphere’s security model, while aligned with modern cloud principles, lacks the flexibility and depth of BW’s traditional RSECADMIN-based authorization framework. This presents practical challenges, especially for medium to large enterprises that require fine-grained access control.

  • Lack of model-level access restriction within a space: For example, suppose you have two analytical models in the same space—one exposing Sales Values, and another exposing Sales Margins a. If the goal is to restrict access to the margin model for a specific group of users, this is not achievable within the same space. The only workaround is to create a separate space for the restricted model and assign user access accordingly. In a Real projects, this leads to uncontrolled space proliferation, resulting in increased administrative overhead and confusion—especially problematic when using a single client for both Development and Production environments. the Business will end up creating 100's space matching each Scenarios. 
  • Even the Data Access Control (DAC) mechanisms cannot enforce row- or object-level restrictions across models within the same space, making governance a nightmare in simple business scenarios.

4) Data Integration Is Still Maturing

While SAP Datasphere offers broad connectivity to various data sources, its integration capabilities—especially with non-SAP systems—are still developing and may not match the stability of SAP BW.

  • Example – MSSQL Extraction Issues: In our experience, data extraction from Microsoft SQL Server, which worked seamlessly and consistently in SAP BW, has been unreliable in Datasphere. Extraordinarily frequent timeout errors occur even when handling moderate data volumes. The same extraction logic, when executed through BW, performs flawlessly. This points to significant performance and stability gaps in Datasphere’s current data integration layer, particularly for external (non-SAP) sources.

entry.title: Re: Implementing Event-Driven Architecture with Canonical Data Model in SAP CPI: Conditional Routing
entry.content: Thanks for the very helpful blog.

entry.title: Re: ABAP EXPORT CHART AS IMAGE FROM EXCEL
entry.content: It's not a question about "Excel files generated by abap2xlsx", it's a question about any XLSX file. If you use OLE or an Excel macro, your code can work only when running in foreground/dialog and if the user has Excel installed on her laptop. So, it's not an ABAP question, not an SAP question. I can find this answer: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/74093484/vba-code-to-export-chart-as-jpeg-file-replicate-save-as-picture-behavior. It generally works via OLE if you convert the VBA code via the classic VBA to OLE rules.

entry.title: System Access
entry.content:
Hi,

Just wanted to ask whether SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance Trial from SAP CAL allows me to create, edit or delete transactions. Currently the credentials provided in the notes (to access the system) forbid the use of any. Please advise, thank you.

entry.title: Re: Adaptation Project for Custom Fiori element application
entry.content: Understood, thanks for the reply. Is there any way to find out if an adaptation project exists using the original Fiori Elements application?

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP - How to Set Attribute Type as Integer When M...
entry.content:
Hi.

 
Neither of the two approaches worked; what worked was converting the AMS value to a string, ending up like this:

 

 
where: '( CriadoPor = $user or Transportadora = $user ) or $user.ams = `1` )'

And in the xs-security.json, I changed the ams attribute type from int to string to make it work in the deployed environment:

"attributes": [
  {
    "name": "ams",
    "description": "ams",
    "valueType": "string"
  }
]

entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Best patches for BI 4.3 SP04
entry.content:
Hello, I encountered a problem with BO4.3SP4 Patch 400.
Why?
The version is not 100% compatible with SAP Datasphere, which requires at least BO4.3 SP4 Patch 600.
So we'll upgrade to 1100, which is more recent and will correct this error.

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Creating a custom AI agent on the BTP
entry.content:
Thank you @prasad_gvk2, The Large Language Model is very instrumental here. It's not just as obvious as in a chatbot.


The LLM for instance takes the user request "Has invoice xyz been paid" and assesses, which of the tools/functions to which it has access, can help answer that request. For this assessment it is using the comments that are within these functions, ie

"""Returns an invoice's status, ie whether it has been paid or not.

Args:
invoice_id: The invoice id
"""

The LLM also produces the Python code, that calls this function, ie with the invoice number from the incoming text

entry.title: DRF IDocs Issue
entry.content:
Hello !

I'm currently working on implementing Data Replication Framework (DRF) in SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) for data replication between two systems:

SENDER SYSTEM: S46 (200)

RECEIVER SYSTEM: S41 (106)

Sender system

Receiver system

I have completed all the configurations. The data are not getting replicated in the receiver system. 

Could you please assist in resolving this issue?

entry.title: Re: Introduction to SAP DRC and Integration aspects
entry.content:
Bonjour,

Nous avons des problemes avec le "peppol" réseau. Je pense qu'il sera fixé eventuellement.

Mais aussi apres ca, nous avons besoin de construire un "ebilling" portail. Il y a plus de vingt "ebilling" portails et plus de cent clients qui reçoivent leur facteur entre ces portails.  Le format est "LEDES" (legal electronic data exchange standard) qui est pour le norme des avocats aux Etats Unis.  C'est possible avec DRC?

Merci - Bruce

entry.title: Accounting entry for Non-Valuated material - stock transfer 301 movement type
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a scenario where Accounting view with valuation class is maintained in material master data for non-valuated material. Due to this, system is showing error for account determination in MIGO movement type 301. 

Now, After maintaining the GL account in OBYC for GBB-VBR, system generates only CO document with ZERO value and do not create accounting document when 301 movement is posted.

Quantity is updated in CO document with respective cost centers of sending and receiving plants.

Requesting to kindly provide your inputs if this system behavior is correct or not.?

Is there any other way to handle this scenario.?

Thank you,

Sumant

entry.title: What Architecture / Porgraming Modell to use when creating a custom Adobe Form
entry.content:
Hi together,

May you could help us to understand the topic better and get the best practice implementation for an S/4HANA 2023 on premise system.

 

The Requirement:

A collegue created an SQ2 View with several Z-Tabels in SAP GUI and would like to have a custom PDF Form for this. Containing our global design (logo, footer, addresses, etc.). There should be a Fiori Elements App with a print button to prin the selected items.

 

Restrictions:

  • SAP System is on premise S/4HANA 2023 AS ABAP 7.58
  • Adobe Form Technology should be used

 

What we have tried so far / ideas we had to solve the problem:

  • Application Programming Model for SAP Fiori

Created a CDS Datamodel based on the SQ2 view. This dat model was exposed as an Business Object (BOPF) with the action print. The print action calls a traditional print programm (FP_JOB_OPEN, CALL <Generated_Form_Function_Module>, FP_JOB_CLOSE. Fiori Elements App with List Floorplan was created. This approach seems to work.
 

  • RESTful Application Programming

Created CDS entity from SQ2 View. Created behaviour definition with action print. Implemented the classic print programm into the behaviour definition, but got a shortdump, that SUBMIT statement is not supported inside of RAP, which is used in FM FP_GET_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME, to get the generated Form. So we had to stop here.
 

Open Questions

  • What about the S/4HANA Output Management and Fragment based Adobe Forms (with ODATA Service as data provider)?

This topic is hard to grasp, in terms of a custom business object, like we have it here, because of the Z-Tables. I understand that SAP implements the connection to the SAP S/4 HANA Output Management into the SAP Standard Application like Goods Movement or Purchase Order and then you can customize it.

 

  • But how to do this with a custom Application like we have it here? Is this even possible?
  • Can we utilize the Fragment based Forms only with the SAP S/4HANA Output Management?
     
  • What about Key User extensiblity Tools?

I read about Key User Extensiblity and saw that you can create your own business objects and CDS views. Is it somemhow possible to connect these to the S/4HANA Output Management and Adobe Fragment based forms?

I hope you could help us to find the correct approach here.

 

Thank you in advance and Best regards

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with SAP Cloud Identity Service with MFA and TOTP
entry.content: Hi Sandeep, In your profile, activate the MFA and scan the QR code from Microsoft Authenticator App.

entry.title: Re: Post missed depreciation for previous period. Is ABAA an option?

entry.title: Issue with FTPO Adapter in Integration Framework 2.0
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am currently working with Integration Framework 2.0 and encountering an issue when using the FTPO Adapter Type.

I am attempting to send an XML file from a local folder, using the FILI atom, to an SFTP server via the FTPO Adapter.

Please find below a description of the scenario:

  • Scenario Overview:

     

  • Structure of the XML file created in the local folder:

     

  • Inbound Atom (FILI): Connection is configured via SLD and tests successfully

     

  • FTPO Adapter Call in BizFlow

     

  • Outbound (Void)

     

After testing the scenario, the following results are shown in the Monitor window:

  • The status is "Completed"
    • However, the XML file is not transferred to the SFTP server.

 

  • When checking the BizIos1 details, the SFTP server parameters appear to be correctly mapped

     

  • The End BizFlow step appears to be empty

     

Additionally, I have verified the SFTP connection using FileZilla and WinSCP, and I am able to create and update files without any issues.

Could you please assist in identifying what might be going wrong?

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Process Chain: load step success if file not found in AL11
entry.content: Any inputs please

entry.title: Re: Bring your own Git for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content: @fabianlupa Rest assured that the feature of SAP Cloud Connector integration is still important; though the argument of legal requirements is a week one (IMHO). If you are allowed to use SAP BTP (and other ABAP-based cloud solutions), you already accepted that your source code is managed by default by SAP in a repository that is not under your direct influence. "Bring your own Git" just opens the possibility to optionally use a different repository provider.

entry.title: Re: Need Help Creating An API Call In SAP Build Process A...
entry.content:
To create an API call in SAP Build Process Automation, follow these steps:

Prerequisites

  1. Service Key: Ensure you have a service key from the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). You can create a service key for the SAP Build Process Automation instance by following the instructions here 

Steps to Create an API Call

  1. Add an API Trigger: You can integrate and manage external applications via API endpoints in SAP Build Process Automation 

  2. Configure API Trigger: Start an instance of your process using an API call, with the inputs for the call configured in the process editor. These inputs can then be used as input fields in your process, for example, in a form or approval form 

  3. Access API Documentation: SAP Build Process Automation APIs documentation is available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. For more information, see SAP Business Accelerator Hub

entry.title: WFA Starter Packages Not Loading with No import logs generating
entry.content:
Hello, 

@YSE-1 

We are loading the advanced starter package for a WFA implementation.  We do currently have a ticket for an OLAP maintenance error on the initial import we found based on another community post so not sure if this issue is related or different. 

When we go to load the adv or starter packages from the implementation kit, the import screen spins.  After waiting some time, we refresh the page and look at the import status.  Nothing appears to be happening with no log outputs or tables generated.  The main home page does say the system is ready for OLAP processing.

Can you let us know if this needs another ticket?

Appreciate your help!

entry.title: Re: PO Creation and Approval timing is wrongly showing in...
entry.content:
Hello @EverrenewEnergy 

Thank you for your observation.

Based on the workflow behavior and timestamp alignment, it is likely that the task attributed to Mr. Sumanth was system-generated, possibly due to a background step or a substitution rule, rather than a manual intervention. In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, such tasks may appear in the workflow log if a user is assigned as a backup approver or if a parallel step is configured in the workflow definition.

To confirm:

  • Please check for any active substitution rules assigned to Mr. Sumanth
  • Review the Workflow Definition to identify any parallel or escalation logic that could involve Mr. Sumanth.

Let me know if you need further support

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Unmanaged RAP Query Implementation: Filter calls identification in if_rap_query_provider~select
entry.content:
the datamodel is the one in my post 😉 the filter dropdown shown in the screenshot is referencing the element company of custom entity ZCE_DELIVERY_STATUS. 

 

io_request->get_entity_id( ).

always returns "ZCE_DELIVERY_STATUS"

entry.title: Re: Support for ASC X12 Subsets (VICS etc.) in Integration Advisor
entry.content:
Hi ,

Thanks for the update! Great to see that ASC X12 subsets like HIPAA, UCS, and VICS are now supported in Integration Advisor and Cloud Integration. The guidance on handling fixed values and codelists is clear and helpful. Appreciate the continued enhancements looking forward to using these new capabilities

Regards ,

entry.title: Re: Exporting System Connections/Tenant List to SAP Cloud Applications Studio new versions
entry.content:
Hello,

did anyone already find a way to use this or a simular method for the new version in Visual Studio 2022? I couldn't find a registry entry referring to the system connections there.

Many thanks

Niko

entry.title: Re: Unmanaged RAP Query Implementation: Filter calls identification in if_rap_query_provider~select
entry.content: you are not answering the first question

entry.title: Re: Lumira server running with errors after updating the...
entry.content: Hi Kiran, I reinstalled, somehow the first installation didn't work. After reinstall the Lumira Server started to work again...

entry.title: Re: Integration of Joule with IAS for SAP SuccessFactors...
entry.content:
it should be same IAS/IPS to be better ie central one for all the users to be synced. then you can single CIS tenant for iAS/IPS which can connect with BTP. Please check here

SAP Discovery Center Mission - Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors

entry.title: Re: "Error in creating team header" in Manage Teams and R...
entry.content: Solved?

entry.title: Re: Restricted Access to Pricing Configuration Elements i...
entry.content:
Hi,

 Please check if the business catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_SD1_PC is added to the business role BR_BPC_EXPERT. 

Regards

Jai Srinivasan

entry.title: Re: Data Transpose in SAP Datasphere using Union Operation
entry.content: Hi @akashsingh1, Thank you it worked.

entry.title: Test plan edit error "A row already exists with this key"
entry.content:
In Solution manager Test Suite we have already test plan. After we have generated test case hierarchy we try to edit test plan. We try to select new test cases and got error "A row already exists with this key". All other sheet are editable this error became only in sheet "Test case selection"

SAP Solution Manager SOLMAN Test Suite

entry.title: Re: Can LMS have separate Login ??
entry.content:
there is a concept for external logins but users should exists in BIZx

Identity Authentication replaced Learning native user authentication and SuccessFactors platform External Learner authentication

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/setting-up-sap-successfactors-learning-with-identity-authentication-and-identity-provisioning-services/sap-successfactors-learning-and-sap-cloud-identity-services-integration-overview

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/importing-user-data-from-platform-with-user-connector-sf/setting-up-sap-successfactors-learning-person-ids-connector

entry.title: Re: Regarding cross Tier , Cross 'ABAP Language" Cloud development
entry.content: Can someone from SAP respond please.

entry.title: Re: Unmanaged RAP Query Implementation: Filter calls identification in if_rap_query_provider~select
entry.content: if they call the same entity? yes. In both cases the implementation of zcl_custom_delivery_status is called. and in both cases io_request->get_entity_id() as part of if_rap_query_provider~select returns "ZCE_DELIVERY_STATUS"
